reviews.review_text:
I purchased this game for full price 11 years ago. The email I used for my Rockstar Social Club, Rockstar Game's third party steam-like software, was hacked and then deleted by the email's domain owner. The email literally no longer exists.

Because I can't get into my Social Club account, I am literally physically incapable of playing the game I own. The licensed, legitimate copy of Grand Theft Auto V I purchased is literally unusable. Both on AND offline. 

[b]I want to clarify, I am disallowed and completely unable to play my game OFFLINE because of Rockstar Social Club.[/b]

People like to grovel at Gabe Newel's feet, claiming that he's the patron saint of gaming, yet Rockstar, Ubisoft, EA, and plenty of other trash companies are forcing Steam users to use a THIRD PARTY SOFTWARE to access the games they purchase on STEAM.

This is profoundly anti consumer, and I am currently locked out of playing three of my Steam games because of Rockstar Social Club.

This is a review for GTA Online, the Story mode is a 10/10

If you are thinking of purchasing GTA for the Online mode, do not do it.

GTA Online is a buggy, extremely abusable mess designed to be as predatory as possible. 1/3rd of players in every public server is modding their game and the community is terrible.

The Gameplay:
Absolutely ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. Nobody is playing anything other than freemode and every other matchmaking-based mode like jobs are completely empty and devoid of players, probably because these gamemodes don't pay for ♥♥♥♥. As for the heists, there's no incentive to do anything other than Knoway Out and Cayo Perico because of the former being free and the latter being the best paying heist in the game.

Due to the game being designed to waste as much of your time doing the same thing over and over again as possible, it only really attracts players who are willing to waste that amount of time to buy vehicles to bully other people in freemode or incentivizes players to cheat instead, and because its so easy to cheat you are at a direct disadvantage for not doing so because EVERYONE ELSE is cheating to some degree, whether that be an actual mod manager that has the ability to kick and crash people's game to just abusing a replay glitch for infinite money. Why spend money on shark cards when the average PC player can just google how to do a replay glitch for Cayo Perico and get 8 million in 30 minutes?

With this infinite amount of money, the most toxic players are the ones with every meta vehicle available, a direct consequence of Rockstar's "game" design of adding stupid powerful vehicles with extortionate prices to get as many shark card purchases as possible from losers addicted to a hyper-realistic power fantasy, unable to understand the concept of basic exploiters having ALL OF THEM at their easy disposal without their profit.

But don't worry! If you are moderately competent with a sniper rifle and have explosive ammo (or have the railgun for a cheap $450k!) you too can completely negate any air vehicle and most land vehicles in general with a few well-placed shots! and with the thermal vision goggles you're guaranteed to keep your hobbies to yourself at the family dinner table when mom's done making dinner.

Modding:
There is no anti-cheat. Despite Battleye, Online is easier to cheat in than any other game I've seen. Some mod menus even abuse Battleye to spoof players' ID so they can manually ban any player they want by cheating with the targetted player's ID as their own. Usually in online games with a cheating problem the ones cheating make the game completely unplayable for others but at least those people know to not have an ego like they're a murder god. GTA Online modders play this game 100% seriously with a mod manager as a safety net for their ego to fall back on and act like cheating in a videogame is some normal part of the game's kit. They'll have god mode and the ability to kick anyone they dislike but unironically Chud out at a level 40 on a Pizza Boy with a wrench

Community:
Most players actually invested in this game are too addicted to the actual base game mechanics to really care about Rockstar's otherwise game-killing balance decisions enough to find a better game to play, which is only really fair to say for GTA:V as it really is a once in a lifetime game, but even most of these players develop a Stockholm complex, acting as if they're noble for enduring Rockstar's terrible game decisions intentionally designed to ♥♥♥♥ them over. GTA Online literally shares the same game philosophy as Roblox, but with grown men instead. That's why its made more than $200m dollars each year this game's been out.

I watched a PvP video of a really skilled player. He was only winning and laughing like he was genuinely enjoying himself, but this passionate enjoyment for the game is mostly overtaken by his hatred for the people he's fighting. He's been doing this same thing for literally 10 years. How does every "vet" player in this game declare total victory in this game and still lose on the mental front? these players are everywhere. Just mad all the time and want something to lash at, because they inherently know the game they've invested their lives to is not designed for their fun or enjoyment like they've always wanted, it is a product to make money.

GTA Online is literal cancer and the fact that this game hasn't been hit by a serious malicious attack yet is jarring considering how cartoonishly easy it is to exploit and abuse this game's spaghetti code. Whatever anti-cheat they once had was removed and deleted because the source code for it was leaked. What kind of multi-billion dollar company handles a situation like that with their 1# product?

With GTA 6's imminent release I can only imagine how fast Online is going to rot after Rockstar puts all focus onto their new cashcow. GTA Online does not deserve your time and effort.

You can get stranded on an island with only this game installed on your computer and after 50 years of surviving still enjoy it as a grandpa on your deathbed.

This is Videogame: The Game.

Very interactive, very detailed, edgy, stylish, perfect music, satirical world.
It's not the best thing ever if we compare specifics but no other "gta killer" can give you what this game can.

Cutscenes alone are so damn entertaining, every character fills the room with their charisma. You can hate Trevor for doing something really bad but you can't take away your eyes from the trainwreck this character is. 

You never skip a GTA cutscene. This game perfectly encapsulates crime drama.

And if you want to go all in and leave a bitter aftertaste then you can play the second part of this package: 
GTA Online.

GTA V Enhanced is a fantastic game—truly fantastic. The addition of Career Mode is a very welcome new feature that the game has long been missing.

Unfortunately, the economy in online mode remains chaotic. On top of that, online mode now resembles a single-player game—there’s no interaction with other players, no chat, and that’s just absurd for a multiplayer game.
Runs good even after emulating it. Just avoid raytracing.

So, this was a phenomenal game. I have still not yet completed story mode but online is one the most fun games i have ever played with my friends. And the best part is that you can do anything you want,  you and your friends could litteraly invade an military base if you guys wanted to. Also, it is so much fun doing heists in this game, although it can get repetitive doing heists over and over again. The first few times doing them are always so much fun to do and when completing them feels so rewarding. Now we are kinda getting bored and are slowly quitting the game, but we had a lot of fun playing online and hopefully story mode will be really good aswell
its gta ofc its the best anyways add sex scene where trevor and franklin

Hark! I have traversed the sun-scorched streets and lawless avenues of a most scandalous digital realm known as Grand Theft Auto V. Verily, this creation is no mere pastime, but rather a sprawling theatre of chaos, villainy, and unchecked debauchery most foul.

Upon entering the accursed city of Los Santos, I was greeted not by virtue nor civility, but by rogues, brigands, and men of questionable moral constitution. One assumes the role of not one, but three distinct scoundrels, each more unhinged than the last. There is Michael, a weary knave of wealth; Franklin, a cunning youth seeking fortune; and Trevor… good heavens, Trevor! A madman so deranged he would make even the village drunkard appear a scholar and gentleman.

The world itself doth teem with life and absurdity. Citizens wander the streets uttering nonsense, carriages of iron roar through the roads at impossible speed, and the constables possess an almost supernatural ability to materialize whenever one commits even the smallest misdemeanor. Steal but a humble wagon, and suddenly an army descendeth upon thee as though thou hadst declared war upon the Crown itself!

Yet, dear reader, despite its wickedness, I confess this entertainment possesseth a most intoxicating charm. The pursuits are endless! One may partake in grand heists, engage in contests of sport, pilot winged machines through the heavens, or simply wander the countryside causing unparalleled disorder. Indeed, never before have I witnessed such freedom bestowed upon a player, though perhaps mankind was never meant to wield it.

The visual craftsmanship is wondrous beyond measure. The sunsets bathe the land in golden light, the oceans shimmer like polished sapphire, and the mountains stand proud as ancient titans. At times, I found myself forgetting my villainous deeds simply to admire the scenery like a travelling poet.

But lo! This world is not without darkness. The violence is abundant, the language most uncouth, and the inhabitants display a moral compass that spins like a cursed weather vane. Sensitive souls and persons of delicate temperament may find themselves clutching their pearls in utter horror.

And then there is the realm known as GTA Online—a chaotic gathering place where players from across the globe unite to either accomplish great feats or torment one another with infernal flying contraptions armed with explosive weaponry. Truly, it is both mankind’s greatest achievement and its final warning.

Would I recommend this Grand Theft Auto V? Aye, though with caution. For those seeking liberty, madness, and spectacle beyond imagination, this title is as a banquet fit for kings. But to those who value peace, order, and preservation of sanity, I say this: keep thy distance, lest Los Santos consume thy very soul

Its pretty good i enjoy it whenever i feel bored! although people who mod the game hate it due to it having sorts of issues like the directory changes and making all mods not work in this version, if you are here just for the joy of playing story-mode and online this is a good game none the less.

I've honestly tried giving GTA Online Enhanced a chance, but this game is becoming impossible to enjoy. Half the time I can't even get into Online. I'll sit through a loading screen just for the anti-cheat to kick me out before I even get to play. Then I have to restart the whole thing and hope the game doesn't freeze on another loading screen. The most annoying part is that I don't even use mods or cheats. My game is completely clean, yet I get kicked constantly for no reason. Meanwhile, people who are actually cheating seem to stay in sessions just fine. What finally pushed me over the edge was getting kicked during a heist right before we got paid. Me and the team did all that work, got to the end, and then the anti-cheat removed me from the game. I got absolutely nothing for the time I put into it. At this point, I can't even play for an hour without wondering when the next random kick is coming. It's frustrating because when the game actually works, it's fun. But spending more time fighting the anti-cheat and loading screens than actually playing isn't worth it. Would I recommend this game? No. Not in its current state. If you're a new player, be prepared to deal with constant issues, random kicks, and wasted time.  Update (June 4th) tried playing the Heist again and same thing it kicked me out right at the end and gave me the default pay instead of 50% this game ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ sucks.

Rockstar Games Services is suuuuucks, I've been doing the exactly same mission for multiple times just because of being kicked out from the session due to ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ server problem and save failed issues. Please fix this ♥♥♥♥♥♥ connection problem already, severely affecting my gaming experience!!!!!!

Grand Theft Auto V Enhanced is essentially a polished and improved version of Grand Theft Auto V Legacy. The main goal here is pretty straightforward: upgrade the visuals, improve consistency, and refine overall presentation without changing what the game already is at its core. It’s still the same GTA V, just cleaned up and modernized, with a few added features and technical upgrades that take better advantage of newer hardware on consoles and PC.

On the technical side, the improvements are noticeable right away. Lighting is better, textures are sharper, draw distances are increased, and asset streaming feels more efficient. The world looks clearer and more detailed overall, and transitions across the map feel smoother with fewer interruptions. On a mid-range PC, performance tends to sit around 60–70 FPS on high to ultra settings, with drops to roughly 40–45 FPS in heavier moments like large firefights, dense city areas, or extreme weather events such as sandstorms. With an SSD, load times are also significantly improved compared to older versions, making everything feel a lot more responsive.

Gameplay itself hasn’t really changed. The core systems are still exactly what they were in the original release. You can play through the story missions in a structured way or ignore them completely and just roam around causing chaos in the sandbox. That freedom is still the main appeal of GTA V, and it hasn’t been altered here. Driving and shooting mechanics are unchanged as well, just presented with a cleaner and more visually advanced look.

Online is still included and remains very similar to the original version, with the same overall structure plus some additional activities and modes. It’s more stable in some areas, and performance issues that used to show up in certain situations are less noticeable. There’s still plenty to do, whether you’re playing solo or with others, and it continues to be a major part of the game’s long-term appeal.

There are still a few limitations. Some systems can feel a bit scripted or rigid at times, and it doesn’t really push the formula forward in any major way. But given the scale of the game, that doesn’t really take away from the overall experience.

In the end, GTA V Enhanced is basically a cleaned-up, more modern version of an already strong game. It doesn’t reinvent anything, but it does make everything look and run better, which is really what it’s aiming for. It’s an easy recommendation for fans of the series who want a more stable and visually upgraded experience, and also for new players who are just getting into it for the first time.

9/10. A solid pick for anyone who enjoys open-world sandbox games, story-driven missions, and a mix of single-player and online gameplay, especially if you want a sharper, smoother version of GTA V overall.

Grand Theft Auto V Online continues to be an incredibly engaging and dynamic multiplayer experience. The vast open world of Los Santos offers endless opportunities for creativity, from high-speed races and cooperative missions to chaotic free-roam antics with friends. The regular updates and new content Rockstar provides keep the gameplay fresh and exciting, whether you're into heists, businesses, or simply exploring the diverse environments.

The community around GTA Online is vibrant, and the game's blend of action, strategy, and social interaction makes it a standout in the online gaming world. The graphics and attention to detail are still impressive, even years after its initial release, which is a testament to the developers' dedication.

That said, as much as I love everything GTA Online has to offer, I’m really looking forward to the release of GTA 6. The promise of a new story, enhanced mechanics, and next-gen graphics has me eagerly anticipating what Rockstar will bring to the table. It’s exciting to imagine how the next installment will build on the foundations of GTA V and push the series even further. Until then, I’ll definitely keep enjoying all that GTA Online has to offer!

Movement-Slop, boring missions, and repetitive content. There was a point in my life where I actually loved the game, but now it feels like just aimless grinding for content that is frankly disappointing. 
Sure, some heists have some form of story, and some characters have a tiny bit of personality, but everything else is so barren of an actual direction/plot. 

On the bright side, some properties and vehicles are actually pretty awesome (the Delorean, Warstock/Military vehicles, Mansions, and Doomsday Facilities). Pavel and the Kosatka Submarine are my two favourite parts of the game. 

Other than that, the community is still quite annoying, the gameplay is repetitive, the story direction is bland, new updates are underwhelming, missions are a drag, and worst of all:

The movement system still sucks ass. 12 years, and the same ♥♥♥♥♥♥ animations and controls are still here.

If you grind this game for hours, you're a dumbass (like me). 
If you spend real money on Shark Cards, you're possibly the biggest dumbass to walk this earth.

Play this game casually, relive some memories, and delete. I assure you the new content is not worth 10 hours of your life, not to mention the close to 50 I've spent after reinstalling this game in 2026. 
GTAO used to be a chaotic playground full of varied, frenzied fun, and a competitive and dynamic player base. Now it's just Shark Card bait, and Rockstar is shameless about it.
It is the same as GTAV legacy but with more features and it runs so much better. The only thing I don't like is the removed text chat for online.

It’s crazy that it’s 2026 and I’m just now fully finishing this game. Back in 2013, I got around 70–80% through the story before moving on to GTA Online and RP servers.

Even now, this game holds up insanely well. The story was great, a little confusing at first with the 3 playable characters and separate storylines, but they all tie together really well as the game goes on.

The gameplay is still fun, the side missions never felt like a chore, and the game both looks and runs great even in 2026.

Overall, I enjoyed this game a lot. Easy 9/10 for me, maybe even a 10/10. Highly recommend.

[h1]GTA+[/h1]: Do not spend money on this. It's a monthly subscription added to a game that is 13 years old. Because they aren't happy enough ripping you off by selling shark cards. Now they offer you the pleasure of ripping you off on a monthly basis. There are constant updates saying "get this car for free! with a GTA+ subscription!". Oh so I just have to pay real money to get this car for free? WOW what an amazing offer! The subscription isn't necessary to play online or offline. The fact they have added this on top of shark cards shows just how scummy the company is. Of course there are fans who will defend rockstar to the death, they are also the same people who get robbed blind by rockstar so who gives a sh*t about them.
Super laggy survers, the game has constant glitches
every. session. has. a. modder. if gta6 doesn't fix this issue the game won't be worth buying.

Only played the story mission. It's classic and fun. But I think it could've been much better, even by the standards of its time. There are a lot of areas and systems that feels underutilized, underdeveloped, just incomplete. There are some bugs here and there, even 13 years later. But it's still a very fun time. 

If you're interested in mods, I do not recommend this version. Legacy has infinitely better mod support. But other than that, this is the better choice.

Great game. I really liked how the main story managed to be both funny and serious at the same time. I've been playing GTA Online for the past few months and have bought almost every business, but now it feels incredibly repetitive. I honestly don't know how people keep doing the same heists or missions over and over again—I've only done them two or three times because they get boring after the second run. Since everything has become so formulaic, I'm thinking of quitting GTA Online very soon. The grind has just lost its spark.
If you aren't crazy enough to run the same heist over and over just to buy a mansion or a business, don't expect anything extraordinary from GTA Online. However, you will thoroughly enjoy Story Mode.
Rockstar, get your arses up and fix the social club error problem. I own this game. How dare you allow a glitch to stop me from playing the game I purchased.

Grand Theft Auto V is still one of the coolest games I’ve ever played. The graphics look amazing, the story keeps you hooked, and the acting feels surprisingly real and cinematic. Every mission feels exciting, and the game is honestly very addictive in the best way possible. I can’t believe I almost finished the game in just 3 days. Definitely a masterpiece that’s easy to get lost in for hours. Looking forward to Grand Theft Auto VI though.

Easily my favorite GTA of all time! (been playing since GTA 3) The campaign was fantastic, and the multiplayer adds so much replay value. It amazes me that they're still updating this game after 10 years. I would HIGHLY recommend playing with friends, but solo works too.

I know this game is popular for it's mods and RP mode (which I can't speak on because I prefer vanilla) but it's full of so much content already, so it will definitely keep you busy.

If you're new to GTA, and if you ever find this game on sale, GET IT! 10/10

GTA V Enhanced takes an already amazing game and makes it look and feel even better. The graphics improvements are noticeable, the city feels more alive, and performance has been smooth for me. Whether you're causing chaos in Los Santos, grinding online, or just driving around enjoying the scenery, there's always something to do.

Even after many years, GTA V remains one of the best open-world games ever made. The Enhanced version is a great way to experience it in 2026.

27 hours played and still having fun. 🚗💥⭐
so fun, gets boring after a while when u do alot of missions, but alot of other things are fun and enjoyable
This is the best game ever. Super realistic and nice storyline, I also like Chop.
I've always liked the game and the improvements are welcome on this version and I appreciate the offer to migrate an online character. I decided to start from the beginning.

I remember when I was a young boy and I remembered the nightclub and all its modifications. I knew I would never be able to get to that level, so I imagined when I would be able to buy whatever I want, max it out and buy anything.

Thats me now, I have every property and every upgrade for it. I've played on and off for a while, taking a long break during the 2020s and then coming back late 2025. This games great and I truly believe it is in the gaming hall of fame for games, GTA VI is supposedly coming out in November of 2026 as of typing this, but we all know it's probably going to be delayed. If it isnt: GTA V had a good long run for over a decade, playing it after school and growing up next to it will always be with me forever. I've loved this game and I always will, I imagine when I'm a billionaire in GTA VI, I'll come back to GTA V, play a few of the last missions and take a tour of the online servers in my mansion, my bunker, facility etc.

Get it when its on sale, its toxic, its horrible, its great, its everything all at once. Something will happen that makes you want to quit but you realise its just a game. When you're a low level; you get spammed 20 times by an orbital cannon or an Oppressor MK2 and you ragequit. Now you're the person in the facility and on the bike, you can do whatever you want and its quite warming.

!!Check if you have access to your rockstar account before buying this game. Rockstar does not readily restore access to accounts if you lose access to your email used. In addition, do not launch this game if you don't have access to your rockstar account, it will idle on a screen prompting you for credentials. This counts as playing time and steam WILL NOT REFUND you if you go over 2 hours!!
